questions.question: What does @ControllerAdvice do?
options: It is a controller that provides advice to other controllers about routing
It marks a class that handles exceptions globally across all controllers
It adds validation logic to every @RequestBody in the application
It is a Spring Security annotation that restricts controller access

questions.question: A ResourceNotFoundException is thrown from a service method. Nothing catches it. What does Spring Boot return by default?
options: 200 OK with null body
404 Not Found automatically — Spring knows RuntimeException means 404
500 Internal Server Error with a stack trace (or generic error message)
The application restarts automatically

questions.question: What is ProblemDetail in Spring Boot 3?
options: A debugging tool that prints detailed stack traces to the console
A standard error response format following RFC 9457 (Problem Details for HTTP APIs)
An annotation that provides detailed information about a @RestController
A Spring Boot Actuator endpoint showing application health details

questions.question: Which exception does Spring throw when @Valid fails on a @RequestBody parameter?
options: ValidationException
ConstraintViolationException
MethodArgumentNotValidException
IllegalArgumentException

questions.question: Why should the catch-all @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class) log the exception but send a generic message to the client?
options: Clients cannot parse stack traces in HTTP responses
Spring requires all responses to be under 1KB
Stack traces leak internal implementation details and can be a security risk
The catch-all handler runs in a separate thread and cannot access the exception message
